description_long: This object is a small, rectangular cardboard box with a light green exterior and white base, commonly used for packaging medicine. The box features printed text on multiple sides, including product information, instructions, and what appears to be a brand name 'LECTOR' with a circular emblem beneath it. The text is primarily white, with some blue and black elements, and the box includes a visible expiration date stamped in blue ink. The surface of the box is matte and appears to be made of thin, lightweight cardboard, typical for over-the-counter pharmaceutical products. The box is sealed, with no visible openings or tamper-evident features on the exterior. Its compact size and clear labeling make it suitable for display or storage in pharmacies, clinics, or urban first-aid stations.
description: A small, light green cardboard medicine box with white base, printed text, and a circular emblem, featuring product and dosage information.
description_view_0: The top of the box displays the brand name 'LECTOR' and a circular emblem, with a light green background.
description_view_1: One side of the box shows dense white text, including instructions and an expiration date stamped in blue.
description_view_2: The bottom of the box is plain white with a visible seam and a blue-stamped expiration date.
description_view_3: Another side of the box features more white text, including usage instructions and product details.
attribute_label_text: LECTOR [illegible] ANTIACID GRANULES [illegible] To be taken as directed by the physician. Store in a cool, dry place. Keep out of reach of children. Batch No: [illegible] Mfg. Date: [illegible] Exp. Date: 10 05 2029
